zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard FitzGilbert de Clare於1090年出生在英國肯特托布利其時，他的父親Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford25歲，母親Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont26歲。他和Adeliza Meschines de Chester至少有5個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1136年4月15日在威爾士蒙姆爾利藍尼 普利馬 普利奧利享年46歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特托布利其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard FitzGilbert de Clare于1090年出生在英国肯特托布利其时，他的父亲Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford25岁，母亲Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont26岁。他和Adeliza Meschines de Chester至少有5个儿子和4个女儿。他去世于1136年4月15日在威尔士蒙姆爾利藍尼 普利馬 普利奧利享年46岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特托布利其。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard FitzGilbert de Clare was born in 1090, in Tonbridge, Kent, England, his father, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, was 25 and his mother, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, was 26. He had at least 5 sons and 4 daughters with Adeliza Meschines de Chester. He died on 15 April 1136, in Llanthony Prima Priory, Monmouthshire, Wales, at the age of 46, and was buried in Tonbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales, Unite: Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard FitzGilbert de Clare est né en 1090, à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles avec Adeliza Meschines de Chester. Il est décédé le 15 avril 1136, à Llanthony Prima Priory, Monmouthshire, Galles, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard FitzGilbert de Clare 1090 in Tonbridge, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford 25 und seine Mutter Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont 26. Er hatte mit Adeliza Meschines de Chester mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 15. April 1136 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Llanthony Prima Priory, Monmouthshire, Wales und wurde in Tonbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard FitzGilbert de Clare è nato il nel 1090 nel Tonbridge, Kent, England, il suo padre, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie con Adeliza Meschines de Chester. Morì il 15 aprile 1136, nel Llanthony Prima Priory, Monmouthshire, Galles, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Tonbridge, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard FitzGilbert de Clareが1090年にイギリスケントトンブリッジで生まれた時，父Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertfordは25歳，母Adelize en Beauvais de Clermontは26歳であった。彼はAdeliza Meschines de Chesterとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1136年4月15日にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアランソニー プリマ プリオリーで46歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントトンブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard FitzGilbert de Clare(이)가 1090년 영국 켄트 토브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Adeliza Meschines de Chester(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1136년 4월 15일 웨일스 몬모우스실르 랜서니 프리마 프리오리에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 토브리지에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard FitzGilbert de Clare nasceu em 1090, em Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas com Adeliza Meschines de Chester. Ele faleceu em 15 de abril de 1136, em Llanthony Prima Priory, Monmouthshire, Gales, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard FitzGilbert de Clare родился в 1090 г. в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, 26. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери от Adeliza Meschines de Chester. Он умер 15 апреля 1136 г. в местности ллантони прима приори, монмоутшир, Уэльс в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard FitzGilbert de Clare nació en 1090, en Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas con Adeliza Meschines de Chester. Murió el 15 de abril de 1136, en Llanthony Prima Priory, Monmouthshire, Gales, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultado en Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
